sanskrit:
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन्।
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया॥
transliteration: kathaṃ vidyām ahaṃ yogiṃs tvāṃ sadā paricintayan | keṣu keṣu ca bhāveṣu cintyo’si bhagavanmayā ||
literal_meaning: How / may I know / I / O yogi / You / always / while thinking / in which / in which / and / states / to be thought / You are / O Lord / by me
contextual_meaning: Arjuna asks for a concrete, repeatable inner technology to keep the Supreme in awareness; he moves from awe to pragmatic need for a working image of the Divine that fits ordinary cognition.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to Krishna now seeks operationalization—translating peak experience into daily working memory cues.
trigger_scenario: After a life-changing insight you ask your mentor, 'How do I keep this alive when I’m back in traffic, spreadsheets, and toddler tantrums?'
anchor_text: Keep the Divine in mind in every thought and moment.
